page: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor allocates exactly one thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled, and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization, AVX, AES cryptography, and FMA instructions, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions.

The instance is equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB SSD. For network connectivity, the server provides a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance split between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, the 32 dedicated cores deliver average multi-core performance, as evidenced by middle-50% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput also perform within the average tier.

Given its dedicated resource allocation and balanced resource density, the b2-120 offers a stable, predictable hardware profile for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and moderate database operations. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or intensive LLM text generation, where its single-core performance and lack of GPU acceleration present clear bottlenecks.
description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 32 dedicated x86_64 Intel cores running at 2.0 GHz without hyperthreading. It is configured with 120.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 400 GB local SSD, and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface. While single-core benchmarks place the server in the lower percentiles, its 32 dedicated physical cores deliver average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark suites. Memory bandwidth, static web serving, and database operations also align with mid-tier performance standards. This hardware profile makes the b2-120 a reliable option for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations, though it is less suited for single-threaded tasks or GPU-dependent workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose KVM server with 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage, optimized for predictable multi-threaded processing and web serving.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated x86_64 Intel cores with no hyperthreading
120 GB RAM and 400 GB local SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Average multi-core performance across standard CPU and memory benchmarks
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and moderate databases
